tools.name: git_add
tools.description: Stage files for commit. Add file contents to the staging area (index) to prepare for the next commit.

tools.name: git_blame
tools.description: Show line-by-line authorship information for a file, displaying who last modified each line and when. For large files, use startLine/endLine to limit output.

tools.name: git_branch
tools.description: Manage branches: list all branches, show current branch, create a new branch, delete a branch, or rename a branch.

tools.name: git_changelog_analyze
tools.description: Gather git history context (commits, tags) and structured review instructions to support LLM-driven changelog analysis. Changelog file should be read separately; this tool provides the supporting git data and analysis framework. Pass one or more review types to control what kind of analysis to perform.

tools.name: git_checkout
tools.description: Switch branches or restore working tree files. Can checkout an existing branch, create a new branch, or restore specific files.

tools.name: git_cherry_pick
tools.description: Cherry-pick commits from other branches. Apply specific commits to the current branch without merging entire branches.

tools.name: git_clean
tools.description: Remove untracked files from the working directory. Requires force flag for safety. Use dry-run to preview files that would be removed.

tools.name: git_clear_working_dir
tools.description: Clear the session working directory setting. This resets the context without restarting the server. Subsequent git operations will require an explicit path parameter unless git_set_working_dir is called again.

tools.name: git_clone
tools.description: Clone a repository from a remote URL to a local path. Supports HTTP/HTTPS and SSH URLs, with optional shallow cloning.

tools.name: git_commit
tools.description:
Create a new commit with staged changes in the repository. Records a snapshot of the staging area with a commit message.

**Commit Message Format:**
Pass commit messages as JSON string parameters. Multi-line messages are supported using standard JSON string escaping.

**Examples:**
- Single line: { "message": "feat: add user authentication" }
- Multi-line: { "message": "feat: add user authentication\n\nImplemented OAuth2 flow with JWT tokens.\nAdded tests for login and logout." }

Note: Do not u

tools.name: git_diff
tools.description: View differences between commits, branches, or working tree. Shows changes in unified diff format.

tools.name: git_fetch
tools.description: Fetch updates from a remote repository. Downloads objects and refs without merging them.

tools.name: git_init
tools.description: Initialize a new Git repository at the specified path. Creates a .git directory and sets up the initial branch.

tools.name: git_log
tools.description: View commit history with optional filtering by author, date range, file path, or commit message pattern.

tools.name: git_merge
tools.description: Merge branches together. Integrates changes from another branch into the current branch with optional merge strategies.

tools.name: git_pull
tools.description: Pull changes from a remote repository. Fetches and integrates changes into the current branch.

tools.name: git_push
tools.description: Push changes to a remote repository. Uploads local commits to the remote branch.

tools.name: git_rebase
tools.description: Rebase commits onto another branch. Reapplies commits on top of another base tip for a cleaner history.

tools.name: git_reflog
tools.description: View the reference logs (reflog) to track when branch tips and other references were updated. Useful for recovering lost commits.

tools.name: git_remote
tools.description: Manage remote repositories: list remotes, add new remotes, remove remotes, rename remotes, or get/set remote URLs.

tools.name: git_reset
tools.description: Reset current HEAD to specified state. Can be used to unstage files (soft), discard commits (mixed), or discard all changes (hard).

tools.name: git_set_working_dir
tools.description: Set the session working directory for all git operations. This allows subsequent git commands to omit the path parameter and use this directory as the default.

tools.name: git_show
tools.description: Show details of a git object (commit, tree, blob, or tag). Displays commit information and the diff of changes introduced.

tools.name: git_stash
tools.description: Manage stashes: list stashes, save current changes (push), restore changes (pop/apply), or remove stashes (drop/clear).

tools.name: git_status
tools.description: Show the working tree status including staged, unstaged, and untracked files.

tools.name: git_tag
tools.description: Manage tags: list all tags, create a new tag, or delete a tag. Tags are used to mark specific points in history (releases, milestones).

tools.name: git_worktree
tools.description: Manage multiple working trees: list worktrees, add new worktrees for parallel work, remove worktrees, or move worktrees to new locations.

tools.name: git_wrapup_instructions
tools.description: Provides the user's desired Git wrap-up workflow and instructions. Returns custom workflow steps (if configured) or default best practices for reviewing, documenting, and committing changes. Includes current repository status to guide next actions.
